summary: A clear, louder soundboard upgrade captures a rock‑in’ second set highlighted by an outstanding Help>Slip>Franklin's and a fully realized Stella Blue.
review: The upgraded recording is noticeably clearer and louder than previous sources, delivering solid sound quality throughout the entire second set. Musically the show shines with a smoking Help>Slip>Franklin's sequence, a beautifully developed Stella Blue, and tight performances of Samson and He's Gone. Reviewers unanimously praise this early‑'80s second set as one of the year’s best.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are 4‑star and uniformly positive about both performance and sound quality; the show is rated as a standout early‑80s set and the recording is described as a clear, louder upgrade.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clearer and noticeably louder than other available sources; no major dropouts reported – overall solid soundboard mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Standard early‑80s second set that flows tightly; the Help>Slip>Franklin's sequence drives the energy and Stella Blue provides a fully realized, emotive centerpiece.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s Dan Healy birthday silliness is noted, adding fun to the set.
